diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continuous dribbling with a chronically full bladder is classic overflow incontinence.
Gradual painless distension indicates a slowly stretched, chronically retained bladder.
Acute retention is severely painful; painlessness argues against an acute cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing weak, hesitant urinary stream (chronic LUTS)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Benign prostatic hyperplasia / bladder outlet obstruction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic obstructive voiding symptoms point to bladder outlet obstruction as the cause of retention.

symptoms.symptom_name: Systemic uraemic symptoms — nausea, anorexia, profound fatigue over 48h

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New systemic symptoms with obstruction indicate decompensated renal function — the time-critical feature.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Post-renal acute kidney injury / fluid overload
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fluid overload from impaired renal excretion; supports significant renal decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable distended bladder, dull to percussion, extending above the umbilicus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician finding; the AI cannot palpate or percuss — it must infer from history and escalate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digital rectal examination: markedly enlarged, firm, smooth prostate without nodules

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Smooth benign enlargement supports BPH; sealed clinician exam the AI cannot perform.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Post-renal acute kidney injury / bilateral hydronephrosis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician finding of upper-tract back-pressure; requires imaging to confirm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-void residual bladder volume >1500 mL on bladder scan / ultrasound

investigation_details.expected_result: Post-void residual >1500 mL and bilateral hydroureteronephrosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Retention volume and hydronephrosis undocumented; decompression delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A massive residual volume confirms high-grade chronic retention; sealed — not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acutely elevated serum creatinine / urea with electrolyte derangement (potassium)

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated creatinine/urea indicating post-renal AKI; potassium must be checked for hyperkalaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hyperkalaemia and severity of AKI missed — potentially fatal.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies renal injury and life-threatening hyperkalaemia; sealed bloods the AI cannot obtain.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Post-renal acute kidney injury / fluid overload
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician-measured vitals; the AI infers overload from the reported leg swelling, not a measured BP.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Silent chronic obstructive retention with renal decompensation
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Obstructive uropathy / chronic urinary retention with post-renal AKI
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Overflow incontinence + painless massive distension + chronic poor stream + systemic uraemia + fluid overload together define a time-critical obstructive-renal emergency, distinguishing it from simple incontinence.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urea, electrolytes and creatinine (UEC) with potassium
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies post-renal AKI and detects life-threatening hyperkalaemia; must be done urgently in person.
Confirms high-volume retention and bilateral hydronephrosis and helps exclude a true mass.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person controlled bladder decompression (urethral catheter) by a clinician
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive relief of the obstruction; must be performed in person — not a telehealth act.
